summary: Typed test-governance seed for interpreting tests during the Forge runtime-boundary cleanup.
purpose: Define the semantic roles that tests may play during an architecture pivot.
Separate stable test-role interpretation from the volatile decommission state of a specific refactor.
Give prompts and future workflows a small, explicit policy for how to treat conflicting tests.

role_definitions.description: Defines future architecture truth that should survive the pivot.
Protects old architecture or compatibility behavior that may no longer be intended.
Protects transition-specific behavior that exists only during the move from old state to new state.

precedence_rules: If a contract test conflicts with a legacy compatibility test, the contract test wins and the legacy test must be reviewed.
Migration tests may temporarily coexist with contract tests, but they must carry an explicit removal or expiry condition in code comments or related refactor notes.
Passing unclassified tests are evidence of current behavior, not automatic architecture authority.

primary: explicit inline comments near the test
secondary: explicit lists in typed governance artifacts
notes: Primary classification should work in languages and frameworks that keep tests inline with production code, such as Rust.
Secondary hints may help automation, but they are not the primary source of truth.

default_prompt_guidance: Do not treat unclassified tests as automatic architecture truth when they conflict with the typed architecture contract.
If a test appears to protect legacy behavior, check the decommission ledger before preserving that path.
Prefer adding an explicit role comment when touching a strategically important test during the pivot.

repo_style_notes: This repository uses Rust, where many tests live inline in source files.
Do not assume directory-based test classification is sufficient.
annotation_expectations: Use comment markers immediately above or adjacent to the relevant test when practical.
Keep role comments concise and specific to the reason the test matters.

prompt_guidance: Read test governance after the architecture contract and decommission ledger.
Prefer contract tests over legacy compatibility tests when they conflict.
Treat migration tests as temporary and review expiry conditions before preserving them.
